channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Over 400 million unwanted and malicious emails were received by businesses in 2024
item.description: Report highlights rising cyber threats, and urges businesses to adopt advanced cybersecurity practices and foster a strong security culture.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Driverless 'bus of the future' is tested in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Commuters in downtown Barcelona have been able to ride the bus for free this week. There’s just one catch: this mini-bus has no one at the wheel.
item.dc:creator: HERNAN MUÑOZ and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Federal Workforce Cuts Threaten Dam Safety
item.description: Federal workforce cuts threaten dam safety as the Trump administration reduces staff at agencies overseeing U.S. dams, raising concerns about reliable electricity, water supply, and flood protection.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SpaceX launches a new crew to the space station to replace NASA's stuck astronauts
item.description: The replacements for NASA's two stuck astronauts are on their way to the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Influencer Brandon Edelman made $768,000 in a year—and what he actually took home
item.description: We all know influencing can pay well—but just how well? Philadelphia-based influencer Brandon Edelman, known online as @bran_flakezz, recently went viral on TikTok after revealing he made $768,000 last year, primarily from brand partnerships and creator funds.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Human firewalls: The first line of defense against cyber threats in 2025
item.description: Now that all the industry thought leaders have weighed in and published their predictions for what the cybersecurity landscape will look like in 2025, it seems that there’s a clear consensus emerging: Both attacks and defense of devices and networks will increasingly be AI-driven as time goes by.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple Original Films will take you behind-the-scenes of a racing cockpit in this new thrilling F1 movie trailer
item.description: The trailer for the new Apple TV+ Original movie F1 shows off Brad Pitt and Damson Idris in a racing comeback story.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: For March’s Patch Tuesday, 57 fixes
item.description: For so few patches from Microsoft this month (57), we have seven zero-days to manage (with a “Patch Now” recommendation for Windows) and standard release schedules for Microsoft Office, Microsoft browsers (Edge) and Visual Studio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Siri’s delay is an artifact of Apple’s unsure AI footing—and more
item.description: Before I go any further, a programming note: On Thursday, March 27 at 1 p.m. ET, my colleague Max Ufberg and I will host “The AI Tools We Love Right Now—and What’s Next,” an online event exclusively for Fast Company Premium subscribers.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Bay Area adds jobs in January while California hiring grinds to a halt
item.description: The Bay Area gained jobs in January, fueled by sturdy gains in the South Bay and East Bay, although the San Francisco metro region lost jobs.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Stupendous Python stunts without a net
item.description: In this week’s Python Report: Sly tricks for setting up Python on a machine with no network, snappier Python code with asyncand await, swifter Python programs with Zig, and slicker web apps with HTMX and Django.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: You can now use an IPv4 address as business collateral - and it could be worth millions
item.description: You can now secure a loan using your IPv4 address as collateral – you’ll even still be able to access and use it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple Music Classical is now available on the web, but its Mac app is still nowhere in sight
item.description: Apple Music Classical has expanded once again and is now available online, but you'll have to wait longer for its native Mac app.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Well, AMD's Radeon RX 9070 series launch isn't going as smoothly as we thought - and it's because retailers have inflated prices
item.description: Color me surprised, AMD's Radeon RX 9070 series GPU prices have already been hiked across multiple retailers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: CAIOs are stepping out from the CIO’s shadow
item.description: In 2016, Andrew Ng, one of the best-known researchers in the field of AI, wrote about the benefits of establishing a chief AI officer role in companies, as well as the characteristics and responsibilities such a role should have.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tech stocks tumble as AI boom fizzles out
item.description: The world’s biggest technology companies have seen their stock prices tumble over the past month as wider tumult in the market hits the tech sector hard after years of steep gains driven by artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Intel under Tan: What enterprise IT buyers need to know
item.description: Intel’s appointment of semiconductor veteran Lip-Bu Tan as CEO marks a critical moment for the company and its enterprise customers. With rising competition from AMD, Arm-based chips, and RISC-V alternatives, Intel faces mounting pressure to defend its x86 dominance.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: INE Security Alert: Using AI-Driven Cybersecurity Training to Counter Emerging Threats
item.description: As Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered cyber threats surge, INE Security, a global leader in cybersecurity training and certification, is launching a new initiative to help organizations rethink cybersecurity training and workforce development.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Social media addicts: Here are tips to stop wasting time on apps
item.description: If you’ve got a smartphone, you probably spend too much time on it — checking Instagram, watching silly TikTok videos, messaging on WhatsApp or doomscrolling on X. It can be hard to curb excessive use of smartphones and social media, which are addictive by design.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Postponed NASA, Space X Falcon 9 mission rescheduled for Friday
item.description: NASA and SpaceX rescheduled the launch of their Falcon 9 rocket rescue mission to the International Space Station for Friday. They were unable to launch on Wednesday due to issues with equipment on the ground, and opted to scrub a Thursday launch attempt due to weather.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: The future of AI isn’t the model—it’s the system
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Intel taps industry veteran as newest CEO in latest comeback attempt
item.description: Struggling chipmaker Intel has hired former board member and semiconductor industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan as the latest in a succession of CEOs to attempt to turn around a once-dominant company that helped define Silicon Valley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This volunteer crew is on a mission to fix Wikipedia’s worst celebrity photos
item.description: Visit a celebrity’s Wikipedia page and there’s a good chance you’ll be greeted by a blurry, outdated, or unflattering photo. These images often look like they were snapped in passing at a public event—because, in many cases, they were.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Intel shares jump after naming chip industry veteran Tan CEO
item.description: Shares of Intel Corp. jumped the most in five years after the company named Lip-Bu Tan as its next chief executive officer, entrusting a former board member and semiconductor veteran with one of the toughest jobs in the chip industry.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: An inside look at Las Vegas valley’s first AI-powered hotel
item.description: Otonomous Hotel, under construction at Russell and Decatur, uses machine learning to create a personalized experience for guests.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, NewsNation

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: DeepSeek
item.description: DeepSeek, founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Chinese entrepreneur, engineer and former hedge fund manager, is generating a lot of buzz — and for good reason. Here are five things that make it stand out (as well as a listing of the latest news and analysis about DeepSeek).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2
item.description: Joe Welch, the deputy to the commanding general of the Army Futures Command, said the characteristics of need helps define the problem set for industry. The post How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
